Title: Apostrophe'
Comment:
Think Boogie Nights vibes set on Venice Beach. The camera fades in to a close-up of skates being laced up. Shots of bodybuilders working out, kites flying, ice cream scoops dripping, lotion being applied, volleyballs being spiked, waves crashing, and our skater beginning his skate down the boardwalk until the music fades slightly at 1:18. At exactly 1:19 we see a close up of a slick trade-off -- money trading place with a dime bag of coke. Our skater spins away and continues a fluid almost choreographed series of transactions. The boardwalk is his corner.

This slick drug trade montage continues until the crash symbol hits at exactly 3:27 as the camera cuts to a bar at the end of Venice Beach and close-up of an ice cold coke bottle being cracked open. Who says business can't be stylish?

Title: Asleep at the Wheel
Artist(s): Working for a Nuclear Free City
Comment:
On a cliff that looked like it was deeply tired of supporting buildings, Tilda Thatch stirred from a nap she hadn’t meant to take. She was an inventor by trade, an outlaw by accident, and currently the only person mildly inconvenienced by Lord Vexmoor’s joy-sucking machine. He had stolen her design, mispronounced her name in a press conference, and now expected no retaliation. That was his first mistake.

As the music kicked in with far too much optimism, Tilda and her assistant Pip scrambled to assemble something vaguely heroic out of springs, old soup tins, and a suspiciously sentient toaster. Nothing made sense, several things caught fire, and at one point the floor disappeared for five seconds. But by the time the chorus hit, they had something that beeped, glowed, and made Pip cry a little. It would have to do.

Title: Peace Somehow
Comment:
Drone view flying over frosted winter trees

Sunlight appears over trees and zooms to Native American warriors on horseback running through the open tall grass. 

Their faces are painted in black as they draw their bows and tomahawks. 

Camera zooms out showing another group running towards the warriors. 

Anticipation builds the closer the two groups get. 
🎵breath in / breath out🎵

Cut to black

Title: Chicago Summer
Comment: This was supposed to have a deep, poetic backstory... but I didn’t have time to write it. I was too busy soaking in geothermal pools, chasing waterfalls, and eating fermented shark in Iceland. Every time I tried to sit down and type something meaningful, a glacier or puffin distracted me. So instead of a story, you get this: a vibe captured in a song, born from the heat of a Chicago summer but released with the chill of Icelandic winds still in my hair.

Title: SPACE
Comment: I was going to have an epic origin story involving stars, solitude, and the infinite cosmos of the human heart—but I didn’t write it. I was in Iceland, standing on lava fields, watching waterfalls ripple across the horizon like the track’s bassline. I told myself I’d get to it after the next hot spring or maybe after the next bowl of lamb stew… but I didn’t. So now, all I’ve got is this: the song’s called "Space"—and I took some.
